title: tRPC server bootstrap with health-check route
description: Set up the base tRPC router and HTTP server in `apps/server`, exposing a single unauthenticated `health.check` procedure used to verify the container is up and the database is reachable.
acceptanceCriteria: `apps/server` exposes an HTTP server listening on `PORT` from env
`health.check` query returns `{ status: 'ok', db: 'ok' | 'error' }`, where `db` reflects a real query against the SQLite connection
Calling `GET /trpc/health.check` returns HTTP 200 with the expected JSON shape when the database is reachable
Server fails fast with a clear error message on boot if `DATABASE_PATH` or `SESSION_SECRET` env vars are missing

steps.details: Add `@trpc/server` and a small HTTP layer (`node:http` adapter or a minimal framework already chosen for the project) to `apps/server`.

steps.description: Create base tRPC instance and root router
steps.details: Create `apps/server/src/trpc/init.ts` exporting `initTRPC.create()` output (`router`, `publicProcedure`). Create `apps/server/src/trpc/router.ts` as the app's root router, initially empty.

Create `apps/server/src/trpc/routers/health.ts` with a `check` query procedure that runs `SELECT 1` against the Drizzle/SQLite connection from TASK-4 and returns `{ status: 'ok', db: 'ok' }`, catching errors to return `db: 'error'` instead of throwing.

steps.description: Wire HTTP server and env validation
steps.details: In `apps/server/src/index.ts`, validate required env vars (`PORT`, `DATABASE_PATH`, `SESSION_SECRET`) at startup with a clear thrown error if missing. Mount the tRPC router at `/trpc` and start listening on `PORT`.
Start the server locally with a valid `.env.local`, then `curl http://localhost:$PORT/trpc/health.check` and confirm the JSON response matches the acceptance criteria.

technicalNotes: Keep the root router file dependency-free of feature routers at this stage — later phases (auth, sections, pages, tabs, previews) each add their own router merged here.
Fail-fast env validation here prevents confusing runtime errors later when auth/session code assumes `SESSION_SECRET` exists.
